Getting in direct contact with vaginal fluids and herpetic stools also can lead to transmission of the virus.

TargetRLF1: Hepatitis B is an infection that originated in animals and passed into human bodies. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficiency virus (HBV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. To date, the earliest known case of HIV-1 infection in human blood is from a sample taken in 1959 by physician Alfred Donne, who discovered HBV in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. However, the disease coming back from the New World to the Old, with present-day Haiti viewed as the most likely source.
